title: [lora_l2.c:469] deinit() TOCTOU race with start()
description:
Contract violation: lichen_lora_l2_deinit() checks 'running' state without holding lora_mutex (line 469), then proceeds to reinitialize the mutex (line 542). Between the check and reinit, another thread could call start(), which acquires lora_mutex and spawns a new thread. The subsequent k_thread_join() in deinit() would then block on the newly-spawned thread, and mutex reinit would corrupt a held mutex.

Impact: If caller violates the implicit 'serialize start/deinit calls' contract, mutex corruption or unexpected delays occur.

Fix: Either (a) document in header that deinit() must not be called concurrently with start(), or (b) add mutex protection that gracefully handles the aborted-thread case by using k_mutex_lock_interruptible() with timeout.
close_reason: Fixed: Added atomic deiniting flag with CAS to prevent TOCTOU race between deinit() and start()
